Therefore not all statistics shown may be available for all countries and for all years. ![Image 139](https://www.itu.int/images/lines/blueline.gif) [](https://www.itu.int/ITU-D/ict/publications/yb/index.html) List of Economies **A**lbania Algeria Andorra Angola Argentina Armenia Australia Austria Azerbaijan **B**ahrain Bangladesh Barbados Belarus Belgium Belize Benin Bhutan Bolivia Botswana Brazil Brunei Darussalam Bulgaria Burkina Faso Burundi **C**ambodia Cameroon Canada Cape Verde Central African Rep. Chad Chile China Colombia Costa Rica Côte d’Ivoire Croatia Cuba Cyprus Czech Republic**D**enmark Djibouti **E**cuador Egypt El Salvador Eritrea Estonia Ethiopia **F**iji Finland France French Polynesia **G**abon Gambia Georgia Germany Ghana Gibraltar Greece Guatemala Guinea Guinea-Bissau Guyana **H**onduras Hongkong SAR Hungary**I**celand India Indonesia Iran (I.R.) Ireland Israel Italy **J**amaica Japan Jersey Jordan **K**azakhstan Kenya Korea (Rep.) Kuwait Kyrgyzstan **L**ao Latvia Lebanon Lesotho Lithuania Luxembourg**M**acau SAR Madagascar Malawi Malaysia Maldives Mali Malta Mauritania Mauritius Mexico Micronesia Moldova Mongolia Morocco Mozambique Myanmar **N**amibia Nepal Netherlands New Caledonia New Zealand Nicaragua Niger Nigeria Norway **O**man**P**akistan Panama Papua New Guinea Paraguay Peru Philippines Poland Portugal Puerto Rico **Q**atar **R**omania Russia **S**amoa Saudi Arabia Senegal Seychelles Sierra Leone Singapore Slovak Republic Slovenia Solomon Islands South Africa Spain Sri Lanka Sudan Suriname Swaziland Sweden Switzerland Syria**T**aiwan-China Tajikistan Tanzania TFYR Macedonia Thailand Togo Trinidad &amp; Tobago Tunisia Turkey Turkmenistan **U**ganda Ukraine United Arab Emirates United Kingdom United States Uruguay **V**enezuela Vietnam **Y**emen Yugoslavia **Z**ambia Zimbabwe _Note:_ Population, main telephone lines, main telephone lines per 100 inhabitants, cellular mobile telephone subscribers and cellular subscribers per 100 inhabitants are also available for economies not shown above. ### ![Image 140: Yearbook of Statistics 2001](https://www.itu.int/ITU-D/ict/publications/yb/images/yb90-99.gif)Yearbook of Statistics 200 1 (No longer available) Chronological Time Series 1990-1999 _27th edition_ The _2001_ _Yearbook of Statistics_ (previously the &quot;Yearbook of Common Carrier Telecommunication Statistics&quot; or &quot;ITU Statistical Yearbook&quot;) features data collected and processed by ITU through questionnaires sent to telecommunication/ICT ministries and telecommunication regulatory authorities. As such, the ITU Yearbook provides the most authoritative source of data about the evolution of the public telecommunication services sector available worldwide. The main features of this edition are: &gt; * Presentation of data in**country tables** allowing readers to view the evolution of telecommunication services in a country. Statistics are provided for the ten-year period 1990-1999. The list of statistics (see below) contained in the Yearbook and the economies (see below) for which there is a page is shown below. &gt; &gt; * Inclusion of selected television broadcasting and information technology time series to provide a wider picture of a country&#x27;s **communication** sector. &gt; &gt; * The Yearbook is available **in electronic form** in Adobe Acrobat (PDF). The data shown in the Yearbook are also contained in the [ITU World Telecommunication Indicators Database](https://www.itu.int/ITU-D/ict/publications/world/world.html) available in the STARS (Socio-economic Time series Access and Retrieval System) data retrieval format, which permits easy selection of the required data and conversion to spreadsheets or ASCII text (**requires use of IBM-PC compatible computer**). _Separate editions in English (paper and on-line), French (on-line) and Spanish_ _(on-line_) _Yearbook online in **English**_**No longer available** _Annuaire online en **français**_**No longer available** _Anuario online en **español**_**No longer available** List of Statistics **Demography, Economy** Population Households Gross domestic product (GDP) Gross Fixed Capital Formation (GFCF) Average annual exchange rate per US$ Consumer price index Telecom equipment exports Telecom equipment imports **Telephone Network** Main telephone lines in operation Main telephone lines per 100 inhabitants % households with a telephone Residential main lines per 100 households % digital main lines % residential main lines Public payphones Waiting list for main lines **Mobile Services** Cellular mobile telephone subscribers Digital cellular subscribers Cellular subscribers p. 100 inhabitants Radio paging subscribers**Other Services** Estimated facsimile machines Videotex subscribers ISDN subscribers ISDN B channel equivalents Telex subscribers **Traffic** Total telephone traffic Total national telephone traffic Local telephone traffic National long distance (trunk) telephone traffic International outgoing telephone traffic International incoming telephone traffic International bothway telephone traffic **Saff** Full-time telecommunication staff **Quality of Service** Faults per 100 main lines per year**Tariffs** Connection fee for telephone service Monthly subscription for telephone service Cost of a 3 minute local call (peak) Cost of a 3 minute local call (off-peak) Cellular connection charge Cellular monthly subscription Cellular cost of 3 minute local call (peak) Cellular cost of 3 minute local call (off-peak) **Revenue** Total revenue from all telecom services Telephone service revenue Mobile communication revenue **Capital Expenditure** Annual investment in telecom **Broadcasting** Television receivers Television licences / households Cable TV subscribers Home satellite antennas **Information Technology** Personal Computers Internet hosts Estimated Internet users **Note**: Availability and coverage varies by country. Therefore not all statistics shown may be available for all countries and for all years. ![Image 141](https://www.itu.int/images/lines/blueline.gif) [](https://www.itu.int/ITU-D/ict/publications/yb/index.html) List of Economies **A**lbania Algeria Andorra Angola Argentina Armenia Australia Austria Azerbaijan **B**ahrain Bangladesh Barbados Belarus Belgium Belize Benin Bhutan Bolivia Botswana Brazil Brunei Darussalam Bulgaria Burkina Faso Burundi **C**ambodia Cameroon Canada Cape Verde Central African Rep. Chad Chile China Colombia Costa Rica Côte d’Ivoire Croatia Cuba Cyprus Czech Republic**D**enmark Djibouti **E**cuador Egypt El Salvador Eritrea Estonia Ethiopia **F**iji Finland France French Polynesia **G**abon Gambia Georgia Germany Ghana Gibraltar Greece Guatemala Guinea Guinea-Bissau Guyana **H**onduras Hongkong SAR Hungary**I**celand India Indonesia Iran (I.R.) Ireland Israel Italy **J**amaica Japan Jersey Jordan **K**azakhstan Kenya Korea (Rep.) Kuwait Kyrgyzstan **L**ao Latvia Lebanon Lesotho Lithuania Luxembourg**M**acau SAR Madagascar Malawi Malaysia Maldives Mali Malta Mauritania Mauritius Mexico Micronesia Moldova Mongolia Morocco Mozambique Myanmar **N**amibia Nepal Netherlands New Caledonia New Zealand Nicaragua Niger Nigeria Norway **O**man**P**akistan Panama Papua New Guinea Paraguay Peru Philippines Poland Portugal Puerto Rico **Q**atar **R**omania Russia **S**amoa Saudi Arabia Senegal Seychelles Sierra Leone Singapore Slovak Republic Slovenia Solomon Islands South Africa Spain Sri Lanka Sudan Suriname Swaziland Sweden Switzerland Syria**T**aiwan-China Tajikistan Tanzania TFYR Macedonia Thailand Togo Trinidad &amp; Tobago Tunisia Turkey Turkmenistan **U**ganda Ukraine United Arab Emirates United Kingdom United States Uruguay **V**enezuela Vietnam **Y**emen Yugoslavia **Z**ambia Zimbabwe _Note:_ Population, main telephone lines, main telephone lines per 100 inhabitants, cellular mobile telephone subscribers and cellular subscribers per 100 inhabitants are also available for economies not shown above. [](https://www.itu.int/ITU-D/ict/publications/yb/index.html) ### ![Image 142: Yearbook of Statistics 2000](https://www.itu.int/ITU-D/ict/publications/yb/images/yb89-98.jpg)Yearbook of Statistics 2000 (No longer available) Chronological Time Series 1989-1998 _26th Edition_ The _2000_ _Yearbook of Statistics_ (previously the &quot;Yearbook of Common Carrier Telecommunication Statistics&quot; or &quot;ITU Statistical Yearbook&quot;) features data collected and processed by ITU through questionnaires sent to telecommunication/ICT ministries and telecommunication regulatory authorities. As such, the ITU Yearbook provides the most authoritative source of data about the evolution of the public telecommunication services sector available worldwide. The main features of this edition are: &gt; * Presentation of data in**country tables** allowing readers to view the evolution of telecommunication services in a country. Statistics are provided for the ten-year period 1989-1998. The list of statistics (see below) contained in the Yearbook and the economies (see below) for which there is a page is shown below. Inclusion of selected television broadcasting and information technology time series to provide a wider picture of a country&#x27;s sector. &gt; &gt; &gt; &gt; * The Yearbook is available **in electronic form** in Adobe Acrobat (PDF). The data shown in the Yearbook are also contained in the [ITU World Telecommunication Indicators Database](https://www.itu.int/ITU-D/ict/publications/world/world.html) available in the STARS (Socio-economic Time series Access and Retrieval System) data retrieval format, which permits easy selection of the required data and conversion to spreadsheets or ASCII text (**requires use of IBM-PC compatible computer**). _Separate editions in English (paper and on-line), French (on-line) and Spanish_ _(on-line_) _Yearbook online in **English**_**No longer available** _Annuaire online en **français**_**No longer available** _Anuario online en **español**_**No longer available** List of Statistics **Demography, Economy** Population Households Gross domestic product (GDP) Gross Fixed Capital Formation (GFCF) Average annual exchange rate per US$ Consumer price index (1995=100) Telecom equipment exports Telecom equipment imports **Telephone Network** Main telephone lines in operation Main telephone lines per 100 inhabitants % households with a telephone Residential main lines per 100 households % digital main lines % residential main lines Public payphones Waiting list for main lines **Mobile Services** Cellular mobile telephone subscribers Digital cellular subscribers Cellular subscribers p. 100 inhabitants Radio paging subscribers**Other services** Estimated facsimile machines Videotex subscribers ISDN subscribers ISDN B channel equivalents Telex subscribers **Traffic** Total telephone traffic Total national telephone traffic Local telephone traffic National long distance (trunk) telephone traffic International outgoing telephone traffic International incoming telephone traffic International bothway telephone traffic **Staff** Full-time telecommunication staff **Quality of Service** Faults per 100 main lines per year**Tariffs** Connection fee for telephone service Monthly subscription for telephone service Cost of a 3-minute local call (peak) Cost of a 3-minute local call (off-peak) Cellular connection charge Cellular monthly subscription Cellular cost of 3-minute local call (peak) Cellular cost of 3-minute local call (off-peak) **Revenue and Expense** Total revenue from all telecom services Telephone service revenue Mobile communication revenue **Capital Expenditure** Annual telecommunication investment **Broadcasting** Television receivers Television licences / households Cable TV subscribers Home satellite antennas **Information Technology** Personal Computers Internet hosts Estimated Internet users _Note_: Availability and coverage varies by country. Therefore not all statistics shown may be available for all countries and for all years. ![Image 143](https://www.itu.int/images/lines/blueline.gif) [](https://www.itu.int/ITU-D/ict/publications/yb/index.html) List of Economies **A**lbania Algeria Andorra Angola Argentina Armenia Australia Austria Azerbaijan **B**ahrain Bangladesh Barbados Belarus Belgium Belize Benin Bhutan Bolivia Botswana Brazil Brunei Darussalam Bulgaria Burkina Faso Burundi **C**ambodia Cameroon Canada Cape Verde Central African Rep. Chad Chile China Colombia Costa Rica Côte d’Ivoire Croatia Cuba Cyprus Czech Republic**D**enmark Djibouti **E**cuador Egypt El Salvador Eritrea Estonia Ethiopia **F**iji Finland France French Polynesia **G**abon Gambia Georgia Germany Ghana Gibraltar Greece Guatemala Guinea Guinea-Bissau Guyana **H**onduras Hongkong Hungary**I**celand India Indonesia Iran (I.R.) Latvia Lebanon Lesotho Lithuania Luxembourg**M**acau Madagascar Malawi Malaysia Maldives Mali Malta Mauritania Mauritius Mexico Micronesia Moldova Mongolia Morocco Mozambique Myanmar **N**amibia Nepal Netherlands New Caledonia New Zealand Nicaragua Niger Nigeria Norway **O**man**P**akistan Panama Papua New Guinea Paraguay Peru Philippines Poland Portugal Puerto Rico **Q**atar **R**omania Russia **S**amoa Saudi Arabia Senegal Seychelles Sierra Leone Singapore Slovak Republic Slovenia Solomon Islands South Africa Spain Sri Lanka Sudan Suriname Swaziland Sweden Switzerland Syria**T**aiwan-China Tajikistan Tanzania TFYR Macedonia Thailand Togo Trinidad &amp; Tobago Tunisia Turkey Turkmenistan **U**ganda Ukraine United Arab Emirates United Kingdom United States Uruguay Uzbekistan **V**enezuela Viet Nam **Y**emen Yugoslavia **Z**ambia Zimbabwe _Note:_ Population, main telephone lines, main telephone lines per 100 inhabitants, cellular mobile telephone subscribers and cellular subscribers per 100 inhabitants are also available for economies not shown above.

<search_documents>
<search query="ITU DataHub mobile-cellular subscriptions per 100 people 1990 country highest" provider="brave">
<document rank="1">
    <title>Mobile-cellular subscriptions</title>
    <url>https://datahub.itu.int/data/?e=POL&amp;i=178&amp;u=per+100+people&amp;c=DEU</url>
    <domain>datahub.itu.int</domain>
    <snippet>Track the digital transformation with the ITU DataHub, featuring hundreds of ICT indicators on connectivity, markets, affordability, trust governance, and sustainability. Find, compare, and download data for nearly 200 economies.</snippet>


  </document><document rank="2">
    <title>UNdata | record view | Mobile-cellular telephone subscriptions per 100 inhabitants</title>
    <url>https://data.un.org/Data.aspx?d=ITU&amp;f=ind1Code%3AI911</url>
    <domain>data.un.org</domain>
    <snippet>2022 2021 2020 2019 2018 2017 2016 2015 2014 2013 2012 2011 2010 2009 2008 2007 2006 2005 2004 2003 2002 2001 2000 1999 1998 1997 1996 1995 1994 1993 1992 1991 1990 ... ITU&amp;#x27;s World Telecommunication/ICT Indicators Database is the main source of global, and internationally comparable, telecommunication/ICT statistics.</snippet>
    <extra_snippet>2022 2021 2020 2019 2018 2017 2016 2015 2014 2013 2012 2011 2010 2009 2008 2007 2006 2005 2004 2003 2002 2001 2000 1999 1998 1997 1996 1995 1994 1993 1992 1991 1990 ... ITU&#x27;s World Telecommunication/ICT Indicators Database is the main source of global, and internationally comparable, telecommunication/ICT statistics.</extra_snippet>
    <extra_snippet>The data are disseminated online, via CD Rom and through numerous ITU publications, including the Yearbook of Statistics and the Measuring the Information Society Report. The statistics are used to track and benchmark the Information Society, to monitor the digital divide and to identify market potential and investment opportunities.</extra_snippet>
    <extra_snippet>It includes time series for more than 140 indicators and around 200 economies. The data are collected directly from telecommunication regulatory agencies and/or ministries and national statistical offices by means of an annual questionnaire, and subsequently verified, harmonized and complemented by ITU.</extra_snippet>

  </document><document rank="3">
    <title>World Fixed and mobile subscriptions data</title>
    <url>https://datahub.itu.int/dashboards/?id=2</url>
    <domain>datahub.itu.int</domain>
    <snippet>Skip to main content · DataHub · About ITU · Regional presence · Connect with ITU · Follow on Twitter · Follow on LinkedIn · Listen in Spotify · Listen in Soundcloud · Listen to Podcasts</snippet>


  </document><document rank="4">
    <title>Mobile cellular subscriptions (per 100 people) | Data</title>
    <url>https://data.worldbank.org/indicator/IT.CEL.SETS.P2</url>
    <domain>data.worldbank.org</domain>
    <snippet>Mobile cellular subscriptions (per 100 people) from The World Bank: Data</snippet>
    <extra_snippet>Free and open access to global development data</extra_snippet>

  </document><document rank="5">
    <title>Mobile cellular subscriptions (per 100 people) - Upper middle income | Data</title>
    <url>https://data.worldbank.org/indicator/IT.CEL.SETS.P2?locations=XT</url>
    <domain>data.worldbank.org</domain>
    <snippet>Mobile cellular subscriptions (per 100 people) - Upper middle income from The World Bank: Data</snippet>
    <extra_snippet>Free and open access to global development data</extra_snippet>

  </document><document rank="6">
    <title>Mobile phone subscriptions per 100 people - Our World in Data</title>
    <url>https://ourworldindata.org/grapher/mobile-cellular-subscriptions-per-100-people</url>
    <domain>ourworldindata.org</domain>
    <snippet>Mobile cellular subscriptions (per 100 people) indicator is derived by all mobile subscriptions divided by the country&amp;#x27;s population and multiplied by 100. For additional/latest information on sources and country notes, please also refer to: https://www.itu.int/en/ITU-D/Statistics/Pages/stat/default.aspx Statistical concept(s): Data can be collected from all licensed mobile-cellular operators in the country, and then aggregated at the country level.</snippet>
    <extra_snippet>Data on mobile cellular subscribers are derived using administrative data that countries (usually the regulatory telecommunication authority or the Ministry in charge of telecommunications) regularly, and at least annually, collect from telecommunications operators. Data for this indicator are readily available for approximately 90 percent of countries, either through ITU&#x27;s World Telecommunication Indicators questionnaires or from official information available on the Ministry or Regulator&#x27;s website.</extra_snippet>
    <extra_snippet>Mobile cellular subscriptions (per 100 people) indicator is derived by all mobile subscriptions divided by the country&#x27;s population and multiplied by 100. For additional/latest information on sources and country notes, please also refer to: https://www.itu.int/en/ITU-D/Statistics/Pages/stat/default.aspx Statistical concept(s): Data can be collected from all licensed mobile-cellular operators in the country, and then aggregated at the country level.</extra_snippet>
    <extra_snippet>A number of countries have fiscal years that end in March or June of every year. Data are usually not adjusted but discrepancies in the definition, reference year or the break in comparability in between years are noted in a data note. For this reason, data are not always strictly comparable. Missing values are estimated by ITU. Mobile subscriptions include both analogue and digital cellular systems (IMT-2000 (Third Generation, 3G) and 4G subscriptions, but excludes mobile broadband subscriptions via data cards or USB modems.</extra_snippet>
    <extra_snippet>Subscriptions to public mobile data services, private trunked mobile radio, telepoint or radio paging, and telemetry services are also excluded, but all mobile cellular subscriptions that offer voice communications are included. Both postpaid and prepaid subscriptions are included. Please cite the International Telecommunication Union for third-party use of these data. ... International Telecommunication Union (ITU), via World Bank (2026) – processed by Our World in Data</extra_snippet>

  </document><document rank="7">
    <title>SUBMITTED May 2006 1</title>
    <url>https://www.itu.int/ITU-D/ict/mdg/material/MDGStory_ITU.pdf</url>
    <domain>itu.int</domain>
    <snippet>subscribers per 100 inhabitants in 1990 to 46.42 in 2004. The most rapid growth occurred in use of mobile · phones. From just 11 million subscribers in 1990, the number of mobile cellular subscribers grew to 1.8</snippet>
    <extra_snippet>subscribers per 100 inhabitants in 1990 to 46.42 in 2004. The most rapid growth occurred in use of mobile · phones. From just 11 million subscribers in 1990, the number of mobile cellular subscribers grew to 1.8</extra_snippet>
    <extra_snippet>The estimated number of personal computers (PCs) rose from some 120 million in 1990 to 777 million in · 2004. Worldwide, PC penetration (PCs per 100 people) stood at 13 at the end of 2004.</extra_snippet>
    <extra_snippet>there are only three fixed lines for 100 Africans, the rate is even lower in Sub-Sahara Africa, where it stands · at 1.6 to one hundred. As a whole, developing countries now account for 58 per cent of all telephone · subscribers in the world, up from just 19 per cent in 1990.</extra_snippet>
    <extra_snippet>fixed telephone line subscribers. One factor contributing to the rapid growth in mobile cellular was the · introduction of second-generation (2G) digital systems, launched in the early-1990s and the high degree of</extra_snippet>

  </document><document rank="8">
    <title>Individuals using the Internet (% of population) | Data</title>
    <url>https://data.worldbank.org/indicator/IT.NET.USER.ZS</url>
    <domain>data.worldbank.org</domain>
    <snippet>World Telecommunication/ICT Indicators Database, International Telecommunication Union ( ITU ), uri: datahub.itu.int</snippet>
    <extra_snippet>World Telecommunication/ICT Indicators Database, International Telecommunication Union ( ITU ), uri: datahub.itu.int</extra_snippet>
    <extra_snippet>Free and open access to global development data</extra_snippet>

  </document><document rank="9">
    <title>Active mobile-broadband subscriptions</title>
    <url>https://datahub.itu.int/data/?e=716&amp;i=11632&amp;u=per+100+people</url>
    <domain>datahub.itu.int</domain>
    <snippet>Track the digital transformation with the ITU DataHub, featuring hundreds of ICT indicators on connectivity, markets, affordability, trust governance, and sustainability. Find, compare, and download data for nearly 200 economies.</snippet>


  </document><document rank="10">
    <title>Mobile cellular subscriptions | Data</title>
    <url>https://data.worldbank.org/indicator/IT.CEL.SETS</url>
    <domain>data.worldbank.org</domain>
    <snippet>Mobile cellular subscriptions from The World Bank: Data</snippet>
    <extra_snippet>Free and open access to global development data</extra_snippet>

  </document>

  <content>
Title: UNdata | record view | Mobile-cellular telephone subscriptions per 100 inhabitants

URL Source: https://data.un.org/Data.aspx?d=ITU&amp;f=ind1Code:I911

Markdown Content:
# UNdata | record view | Mobile-cellular telephone subscriptions per 100 inhabitants
[](https://data.un.org/Data.aspx?d=ITU&amp;f=ind1Code:I911)

[![Image 1: Find UNdata on Facebook](https://data.un.org/_Images/Facebook.png)](http://www.facebook.com/group.php?gid=128384224664)[![Image 2: Follow UNdata on Twitter](https://data.un.org/_Images/Twitter.png)](http://twitter.com/undata)[![Image 3: Please send us your feedback](https://data.un.org/_Images/SendFeedback.png)](https://data.un.org/Feedback.aspx)

[![Image 4: logo](https://data.un.org/_Images/logo_small.png)](https://data.un.org/Default.aspx)

[Datamarts](https://data.un.org/Explorer.aspx)[Update Calendar](https://data.un.org/UpdateCalendar.aspx)[Glossary](javascript:;)[API](javascript:;)[More](javascript:;)

* [About UNdata](https://data.un.org/Host.aspx?Content=About)
* [Advanced search](https://data.un.org/AdvancedSearch.aspx)
* [Explorer](https://data.un.org/Explorer.aspx)

* [Documentation](https://data.un.org/Host.aspx?Content=API)
* [Browse](https://data.un.org/SdmxBrowser)
* [Web Service](https://data.un.org/ws)

**Statistics**

Source

# World Telecommunication/ICT Indicators Database

## Source:[International Telecommunications Union](http://www.itu.int/ITU-D/ict/)

ITU&#x27;s World Telecommunication/ICT Indicators Database is the main source of global, and internationally comparable, telecommunication/ICT statistics. It includes time series for more than 140 indicators and around 200 economies. The data are collected directly from telecommunication regulatory agencies and/or ministries and national statistical offices by means of an annual questionnaire, and subsequently verified, harmonized and complemented by ITU.

The data are disseminated online, via CD Rom and through numerous ITU publications, including the Yearbook of Statistics and the Measuring the Information Society Report. The statistics are used to track and benchmark the Information Society, to monitor the digital divide and to identify market potential and investment opportunities. They are an indispensable tool for policy makers in the area of ICT for development.

[Download the footnotes in Excel format](https://data.un.org/_Docs/ITUFootnotes2010.xls)

Last update in UNdata: 2024/04/04

Next update in UNdata: 2025/04/01
